//: Pairs allowed to exceed the 3% kit↔app parity gate. ONLY semantic/product divergences belong here — never styling drift. Every entry needs a reason and a matching section in REMAINING-DIVERGENCES.md.
deck-settings--move--light.png: Semantic divergence: the kit mock moves decks between decks (Library root / TOPIK Prep / This deck); the shipped domain moves decks between language pairs (moveDeckUseCase). Chrome and row anatomy match; only destination row text differs. See REMAINING-DIVERGENCES.md.
deck-content-choice--default--light.png: Semantic/product divergence: the kit adopts the unified Deck model — a nested deck is a Deck with parentId, not a separate Subdeck object — so the create screen reads 'Organise with nested decks', rendered ahead of the app (which still says 'subdecks'); additionally the branch kit renders the pre-rework single-state deck-content-choice while the app ships the reworked create-deck flow (see the app-only deck-content-choice--named baseline). Choice-card chrome + anatomy match; copy + state model differ. App alignment tracked. See REMAINING-DIVERGENCES.md.
deck-content-choice--default--dark.png: Same as deck-content-choice--default--light: unified Deck-model copy ('nested decks') rendered ahead of the app + pre-rework kit vs reworked app create-deck flow. See REMAINING-DIVERGENCES.md.
